title: श्री मल्लिकार्जुन स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मल्लिकार्जुन स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय मल्लिकार्जुनाय।
श्रीशैलवासिने परमेश्वराय॥

श्रीशैलशृङ्गे विमले प्रसन्नं
मल्लिकार्जुनं शङ्करं वन्दे।
भ्रमरामललिङ्गं महेश्वरं
कृष्णानदीतीरनिवासिनं च॥ १॥

मल्लिकापुष्पसुगन्धवासितं
हारकेयूरकिरीटभूषितम्।
पार्वतीवल्लभमीश्वरं परं
मल्लिकार्जुनमहं नमामि तम्॥ २॥

श्रीशैलमाहात्म्यमनन्तपुण्यं
यत्रार्जुनः पाण्डवपुङ्गवश्च।
तपश्चचार शिवमानसेन
तत्राविरासीद् भगवान् महेशः॥ ३॥

कृष्णावेणीतटे रम्ये नागार्जुनसुसेविते।
पातालगङ्गातीर्थे च मल्लिकार्जुनमाश्रये॥ ४॥

श्रीशैलशिखरस्थं तं भ्रमराम्बासमन्वितम्।
चन्द्रमौलिं जटाजूटं नीलकण्ठं पिनाकिनम्॥ ५॥

गजचर्मपरीधानं सर्पहारं कपालिनम्।
त्रिनेत्रं पञ्चवक्त्रं च दशबाहुं उमापतिम्॥ ६॥

नन्दीश्वरसमायुक्तं भूतगणसमावृतम्।
विरूपाक्षं विश्वनाथं मल्लिनाथं नमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

शिवे शैले महाशैले सह्याद्रौ पुण्यपर्वते।
द्वितीयं ज्योतिर्लिङ्गं वै मल्लिकार्जुनसंज्ञकम्॥ ८॥

ब्रह्महत्यादिपापानि नाशयेत् सर्वकिल्बिषम्।
श्रीशैलदर्शनात्पुण्यं कोटियज्ञफलं लभेत्॥ ९॥

मल्लिकार्जुनदेवस्य स्तोत्रं यः पठते नरः।
सर्वान् कामानवाप्नोति शिवलोकं स गच्छति॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री मल्लिकार्जुन स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mallikarjuna Stotram ||

Om Namah Shivaya Mallikarjunaya |
Shrishaila-vasine Parameshwaraya ||

Shrishaila-shrringe vimale prasannam
Mallikarjunam Shankaram vande |
Bhramaramala-lingam Maheshwaram
Krishnanadi-tira-nivasinam cha || 1 ||

Mallika-pushpa-sugandha-vasitam
Hara-keyura-kirita-bhushitam |
Parvati-vallabham-Ishwaram param
Mallikarjunam-aham namami tam || 2 ||

Shrishaila-mahatmyam-ananta-punyam
Yatra-Arjunah Pandava-pungavash-cha |
Tapash-chachara Shiva-manasena
Tatra-aviraseeed Bhagavan Maheshah || 3 ||

Krishnaveni-tate ramye Nagarjuna-susevite |
Patala-Ganga-tirthe cha Mallikarjunam-ashraye || 4 ||

Shrishaila-shikhara-stham tam Bhramaramba-samanvitam |
Chandramaulim jata-jutam Nilakantham Pinakinam || 5 ||

Gajacharma-paridhanam sarpa-haram Kapalinam |
Trinetram pancha-vaktram cha dasha-bahum Uma-patim || 6 ||

Nandishwara-samayuktam bhuta-gana-samavritam |
Virupaksham Vishwanatham Mallinatham namamyaham || 7 ||

Shive Shaile Mahashaile Sahyadrau punya-parvate |
Dvitiyam Jyotirlingam vai Mallikarjuna-samjnakam || 8 ||

Brahma-hatya-adi-papani nashayet sarva-kilbisham |
Shrishaila-darshanat-punyam koti-yajna-phalam labhet || 9 ||

Mallikarjuna-devasya stotram yah pathate narah |
Sarvan kaman-avapnoti Shivalokam sa gachchhati || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Mallikarjuna Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: श्रीशैल पर्वत के निर्मल शिखर पर विराजमान, मल्लिका पुष्पों की सुगन्ध से वासित, हार-केयूर-किरीट से भूषित, पार्वती वल्लभ मल्लिकार्जुन को प्रणाम। जहाँ अर्जुन ने शिव मन से तप किया और भगवान महेश प्रकट हुए। कृष्णावेणी नदी के रम्य तट पर, भ्रमराम्बा सहित विराजमान, चन्द्रमौलि, नीलकण्ठ, पिनाकी को नमन। यह श्रीशैल पर द्वितीय ज्योतिर्लिंग है, जिसके दर्शन मात्र से कोटि यज्ञ का फल मिलता है। इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से सब कामनाएँ पूर्ण होती हैं और शिवलोक प्राप्ति होती है।
benefits: Equivalent to pilgrimage to Srisailam, the second Jyotirlinga
Traditionally recited for removal of brahma-hatya and all major sins
Invokes fruit equal to one crore yajnas
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires of the devoted reciter
Invokes the blessing of shivaloka after death
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles and grants spiritual progress
description: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the Mallikarjuna Stotram (श्री मल्लिकार्जुन स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Shiva Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to equivalent to pilgrimage to Srisailam, the second Jyotirlinga and destroy Brahma-hatya and all major sins. It is also said to remove obstacles and grant spiritual progress. For best results, devotees chant this on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan Somvar. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Mallikarjuna Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mallikarjuna Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to second Jyotirlinga at Srisailam, Andhra Pradesh.

seoFaqs.question: Which shrine and tradition does the Mallikarjuna Stotram belong to?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mallikarjuna Stotram is dedicated to Shiva worshipped as Mallikarjuna Swamy at Srisailam in the Nallamala hills of Andhra Pradesh, on the south bank of the Krishna river. Srisailam is unique among Hindu kshetras in being simultaneously a Jyotirlinga (Mallikarjuna) and a Shakti Peetha (Bhramaramba), and the stotram tradition reflects this dual identity. Adi Shankaracharya is credited with composing the Mallikarjuna Mangalashasanam during his digvijaya through the Andhra region, and a companion Bhramaramba Ashtakam for the goddess. Together these two compositions form the canonical Srisailam stotra pair recited during the temple's daily and festival liturgies.

seoFaqs.question: What does the name 'Mallikarjuna' mean and how does the stotram reflect it?
seoFaqs.answer: The name combines mallika (jasmine) and arjuna (the white-flowered Terminalia arjuna tree, also a name of Indra). Two traditions explain the compound. In one, it refers to Shiva and Parvati together, with mallika denoting Parvati who offered jasmine flowers and arjuna denoting Shiva. In another, the linga is said to be naturally fragrant like jasmine. The stotram references this floral-iconographic identity, addressing the deity as the one adorned by jasmine garlands, and links it to the Srisaila parvata setting where forest groves of jasmine bloom. This nature imagery distinguishes the Mallikarjuna Stotram from urban Shiva stotras like the Kashi Vishwanath Ashtakam.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Puranic story behind the Mallikarjuna Jyotirlinga?
seoFaqs.answer: The Skanda Purana narrates that when Ganesha was married before Kartikeya, the latter retreated in displeasure to the Krauncha mountain. Shiva and Parvati followed their son to bring him back. Unable to persuade Kartikeya to return, the divine parents took up permanent residence on the same mountain to remain near him. Shiva manifested as the Mallikarjuna Jyotirlinga and Parvati as the Bhramaramba Devi (the goddess of bees). The stotram alludes to this kuladevata-style parental devotion, addressing Mallikarjuna as the one who chose to dwell on Srisaila out of love for his son. This narrative anchor makes the stotram a popular recitation in family contexts and during Skanda Shashthi.

seoFaqs.question: Why is Srisailam considered both a Jyotirlinga and a Shakti Peetha?
seoFaqs.answer: Srisailam is one of only a small handful of kshetras where a Jyotirlinga and a Shakti Peetha coexist within the same temple complex, the others including Varanasi (Vishwanath and Vishalakshi) and Naga (Nageshwar with neighbouring Shakti). At Srisailam, the upper part of Sati's neck is said to have fallen here in the Daksha-yajna narrative, sanctifying the site as Bhramaramba Shakti Peetha, while Shiva manifested as Mallikarjuna Jyotirlinga in the same precinct. Devotional practice therefore requires a paired recitation: the Mallikarjuna Stotram for Shiva and the Bhramaramba Ashtakam for the Devi, in that order, because the Jyotirlinga is approached first in temple parikrama.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Mallikarjuna Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most prescribed occasions are Maha Shivratri (when Srisailam hosts a major brahmotsavam), Karthika masam Mondays (when Karthika Deepam is offered in the temple), Mahanavami of Sharad Navaratri (because of the Shakti Peetha link), Skanda Shashthi (because of the Kartikeya legend) and Ugadi (regional new year). Pilgrims to Srisailam recite the stotram before darshan, and devotees of the Vira Shaiva tradition include it in their daily ishtalinga puja because the kshetra has historic significance for that lineage. Home recitation typically pairs the Mallikarjuna Stotram with the Bhramaramba Ashtakam, observing the temple's own Shiva-then-Shakti darshan order.
